cards.name: The Balance
cards.rules_text:
Equilibrium — Each player's life total becomes equal to the highest life total among all players.
Parity — Each player sacrifices creatures, planeswalkers, artifacts, and enchantments until they control the same number of them as the player who controls the fewest.
Reckoning — Each player discards cards until their hand size is equal to the lowest hand size among all players.
Erosion — Each player sacrifices lands until they control the same number of lands as the player who controls the fewest.
cards.flavor_text: The scales of fate care not for your accumulated power.

cards.name: The Comet
cards.rules_text:
Suspend 2 — {0}{U}{U} (Exile this with two time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter. When the last is removed, you may cast it without paying its mana cost.)
Impact — When you cast The Comet, you may choose target creature you control. If you do, all other creatures phase out until end of turn.
If the chosen creature deals combat damage to a player this turn, it gains flying, double strike, and "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, draw two cards."
If the chosen creature didn't deal combat damage to a player this turn, exile it at the beginning of your end step.
cards.flavor_text: When heavens pierce the veil, one shall rise beyond the stars.

cards.name: The Donjon
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing — When you draw this card, you become dimensionally displaced. Create a Temporal Stasis Sphere emblem and exile all permanents you control until that emblem leaves the battlefield.
Eternal Damnation — You can't draw from the Deck of Many Things for the remainder of the game.
cards.flavor_text: The cruelest prison has no walls—only the endless moment between tick and tock.

cards.name: Curse of Weakness
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
• If a source would deal damage to you or a permanent you control, it deals that much plus 2 damage instead.
• Spells your opponents cast that target you cost {2} less to cast.
• At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 2 life and sacrifice a permanent unless you discard a card.
• Your maximum hand size is reduced by 3.
Eternal Gaze — The Euryale can't be removed in any way except by an effect from a card named The Fates.
cards.flavor_text: The medusa's cursed image lingers, forever petrifying its victim's fate.

cards.name: The Fates
cards.rules_text:
The Fates can't be removed from play by any spell, ability, or effect.
{0}, Shuffle The Fates into the Deck of Many Things: Choose one —
• Rewind — Remove all effects, counters, and modifications applied by a card drawn from the Deck of Many Things. Return affected permanents and players to their state before that card was drawn.
• Deny — Choose a spell, permanent, or card drawn from the Deck of Many Things. Exile it.
• Cleanse — Exile target emblem or curse named Euryal.
• Weave — Look at the top three cards of the Deck of Many Things and put them back in any order. Then, you may draw a card from the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: The threads of fate are woven, unraveled, and rewoven at the whims of the capricious Fates.

cards.name: Vengeful Archfiend
cards.rules_text:
Suspend X — {0}{R}{G} (X is determined by rolling a four-sided die. Exile this card with a number of time counters on it equal to the result. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter. When the last is removed, cast it without paying its mana cost.)
Vendetta — When Vengeful Archfiend enters the battlefield, an opponent chosen at random gains control of it, and you become its nemesis.
Haste, Trample, Menace
Vengeful Archfiend can't block and attacks its nemesis each combat if able.
Torment — Whenever Vengeful Archfiend deals combat damage to its nemesis, put a rage counter on it. When you do, that player discards a card and sacrifices a permanent.
Vengeful Archfiend gets +2/+0 for each rage counter on it.
When Vengeful Archfiend leaves the battlefield or its nemesis loses the game, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: "Your soul bears my mark. Now suffer eternal persecution."

cards.name: The Fool
cards.rules_text:
Misfortune — Sacrifice two nontoken permanents, discard two cards, skip your next draw step, mill ten cards, and lose 5 life.
Draw a card from the Deck of Many Things.
Exile The Fool.
cards.flavor_text: The Fool stumbles not into emptiness, but into fate itself.

cards.name: The Gem
cards.rules_text:
Fortune — When Gem enters the battlefield, create X Treasure tokens, where X is the number of lands in play.
Opulence — At the beginning of your upkeep, create two Treasure tokens.
Liquidate — {T}, Sacrifice Gem: Create ten Treasure tokens.
cards.flavor_text: A fortune beyond measure, yet still finite.

cards.name: The Jester
cards.rules_text:
Mirth — Draw three cards, scry 3, you may play an additional land this turn, create a Treasure token, and gain 5 life. (Treasure tokens are artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Add one mana of any color.")
You may draw up to two additional cards from the Deck of Many Things.
Exile The Jester.
cards.flavor_text: The jester's wisdom lies hidden beneath the mirth and folly.

cards.name: The Key
cards.rules_text:
Shroud
Living Weapon (When this Equipment enters, create a 0/0 black Phyrexian Germ creature token, then attach this Equipment to it.)
Enhancement — At the beginning of your upkeep or whenever equipped creature deals combat damage to a player, put a power counter on The Key.
Equipped creature gets +2/+0 for each power counter on The Key.
Transmutation — {0}: Remove all abilities granted by The Key from equipped creature. Choose two — Haste, Flying, Fear, Skulk, Menace, Intimidate, Double strike, Trample, Bushido 3, Flanking, Battle cry, Provoke, Exalted, Afflict 3, Frenzy 3, Deathtouch, Toxic 2, Prowess, Infect, Wither, or Annihilator 1. It gains the chosen abilities until end of turn.
Equip {0}
cards.flavor_text: The weapon adapts to its wielder's skill, becoming precisely what they need.

cards.name: The Knight
cards.rules_text:
Double strike, Menace
Oath — Other Knights you control get +1/+1 and have vigilance.
Conscription — When Knight enters or attacks, create two 2/2 white and black Knight creature tokens.
Dark Blessing — Pay {X} life, where X is the number of Knights you control: Put that many +1/+1 counters on Knights you control, divided as you choose, and each of those creatures gain lifelink until end of turn. Activate this ability anytime you could cast a sorcery.
cards.flavor_text: "Death refused my oath. Now I serve through eternal undeath."

cards.name: The Moon
cards.rules_text:
The Moon enters the battlefield with three wish counters on it.
Lunar Wish — {0}, Remove a wish counter from The Moon: Choose one —
• Search your library for a card, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
• Target player gains 10 life and draws three cards.
• Cast a spell from your hand without paying its mana cost.
• Exile target nonland permanent.
• Create a 7/7 colorless Spirit creature token with flying and vigilance.
• Return target permanent card from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Return target permanent card you own from exile to the battlefield.
Whenever The Moon becomes tapped, scry 1. (Look at the top card of your library. You may put that card on the bottom.)
At the beginning of your end step, if The Moon has no wish counters on it, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: Your heart's desire manifests in the silvery moonlight.

cards.name: The Puzzle
cards.rules_text:
Tempt Fate — When The Puzzle enters the battlefield, you may draw an additional card from the Deck of Many Things.
Enchant player
Enchanted player plays with their hand revealed and skips their draw step.
Confusion — At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, that player exiles a card from their hand face down. They may look at and play that card as if it were in their hand.
Ineptitude — Whenever a spell controlled by enchanted player resolves, if it wasn't cast from exile with The Puzzle, counter that spell.
Consolation — At the beginning of enchanted player's end step, they draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: The mind, once brilliant, now struggles with even the simplest of tasks.

cards.name: The Rogue
cards.rules_text:
Haste, First strike, Deathtouch, Ward {2}
Skulk (This creature can't be blocked by creatures with greater power.)
Subversion — When The Rogue enters the battlefield, gain control of target creature an opponent controls with the highest power. Untap it. It gains haste until end of turn.
Backstab — Whenever The Rogue deals combat damage to a player, if it wasn't blocked, it deals triple damage instead and that player discards a card. You may cast target instant or sorcery card from that player's graveyard this turn without paying its mana cost.
Betrayal — When The Rogue leaves the battlefield, an opponent at random gains control of target creature you control with the lowest power.
cards.flavor_text: Trust is a currency best spent quickly.

cards.name: The Ruin
cards.rules_text:
Bankruptcy — Sacrifice all artifacts you control, then exile all artifact cards from your hand and graveyard.
Foreclosure — Sacrifice all lands you control. For the next three turns, you can't play lands.
Auction — Starting with the player to your left, each opponent gains control of a non-artifact, non-land permanent you control of their choice until all your remaining permanents are claimed.
cards.flavor_text: Fortunes built over lifetimes can vanish in an instant.

cards.name: The Vizier
cards.rules_text:
When The Vizier enters the battlefield, exile the top seven cards of your library face down. You may look at them at any time.
Meditation — {0}, {T}: Exile the top three cards of your library face down. Look at all cards exiled with The Vizier. You may put one of them into your hand. You may cast that spell this turn as though it had flash. If you do, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy.
Epiphany — At the beginning of your upkeep, put a wisdom counter on The Vizier. Then if it has two or more wisdom counters on it, you may search your library for a card and exile it. Until end of game, you may cast that card from exile without paying its mana cost. (This effect continues even if The Vizier leaves the battlefield.)
Guidance — Whenever you cast a spell that shares a color with a card exiled with The Vizier, scry 2, then draw a card. That spell costs {1} less to cast. If that spell was cast from exile, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy.
cards.flavor_text: "In each moment lies a thousand paths. I have glimpsed them all."

cards.name: Avatar of Death
cards.rules_text:
Flying, Indestructible, Menace, Deathtouch, Wither
Protection from abilities and colored spells
When Avatar of Death enters, mark the player who summoned it and each player's life total is reduced to 10. Then, a random opponent gains control of it.
Avatar of Death attacks the marked player each combat if able and can't block.
When Avatar of Death dies or the marked player loses the game, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things.
Whenever the marked player or Avatar of Death becomes the target of a spell or ability or is dealt damage by an opponent, that opponent creates a token copy of Avatar of Death.
Creatures that die by Avatar of Death are exiled. When Avatar of Death deals combat damage to a player, that player loses the game.
cards.flavor_text: "You mistake my patience for mercy." — Death

cards.name: The Star
cards.rules_text:
Transcendence — Roll two eight-sided dice. If any number is rolled more than once, ignore those rolls and repeat this process. You get enhancement emblems equal to the numbers rolled.
1. Agility: "Creatures you control get +2/+2 and have double strike and vigilance."
2. Charisma: "When a creature enters the battlefield, create a 1/1 white Soldier creature token."
3. Focus: "You may cast spells as though they had flash and they cost {2} less to cast."
4. Fortitude: "Your life total becomes 50 and you have hexproof. During your upkeep, gain 3 life."
5. Intellect: "At the beginning of your draw step, draw two additional cards."
6. Wisdom: "You have no maximum hand size and you may play two additional lands each turn."
7. Wit: "The first spell you cast each turn can't be countered and has cascade."
8. Cunning: "Whenever an opponent casts their first spell each turn, draw a card."
cards.flavor_text: In the star's light, mortal limits fall away.

cards.name: The Sun
cards.rules_text:
Radiance — Draw seven cards, scry 5, you may play up to two additional lands this turn, you have no maximum hand size for the rest of the game, gain 20 life, and create three Treasure tokens. (They're artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Add one mana of any color.")
Illumination — Search target player's library for an artifact card and put it onto the battlefield under your control. Then that player shuffles his or her library.
cards.flavor_text: In the sun's radiance, all knowledge and treasures are illuminated.

cards.name: The Talons
cards.rules_text:
Disintegration — Destroy all enchantments you control. For each enchantment destroyed this way, you lose 2 life.
Unmaking — Exile all artifacts you control. For each artifact exiled this way, target opponent chosen at random creates a Treasure token. Search your library and hand for all artifact cards and exile them.
cards.flavor_text: In an instant, a lifetime's collection of power crumbles to dust, while its greatest treasures vanish into eager hands.

cards.name: The Throne
cards.rules_text:
Rightful Claim — When Throne enters the battlefield, you may search your library for up to two land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle. Create X 2/2 white Human Knight creature tokens with vigilance, where X is the number of lands you control. Then, each opponent creates X 2/2 black Zombie creature tokens, where X is twice the number of lands you control. Zombies your opponents control attack each combat if able.
Throne's Authority — Other non-Soldier creatures you control get +1/+1 and have "When this creature dies, create a 1/1 white Human Soldier creature token."
Royal Influence — {0}, {T}: Gain control of target creature an opponent controls for as long as you control Throne. Untap that creature. It gains haste until end of turn and "When this creature deals combat damage to a player, create two 1/1 white Human Soldier creature tokens." Activate this ability only during your turn.
cards.flavor_text: A true ruler's influence persists long after the battle ends.

cards.name: The Void
cards.rules_text:
Soul Imprisonment — When you draw this card, exile your soul and you become incapacitated. Create a colorless Soul Prison artifact token.
Eternal Damnation — You can't draw from the Deck of Many Things for the remainder of the game.
cards.flavor_text: The soul knows its jailer, but the body knows only emptiness.

cards.name: Deck of Many Things Rules
cards.rules_text:
What is the Deck of Many Things? A legendary artifact of incredible power, chaos, and fate. Drawing from this deck will forever change your destiny — for better or worse.
DRAW AT YOUR OWN RISK!
Setup —
• Shuffle the Deck of Many Things and place it in the command zone.
• Each player starts with 1 Fate Point and can only have 1 Fate Point maximum.
• Optional Rule: Each player may draw one non-declared card from the Deck of Many Things at the start of the game without spending a Fate Point.
When You Can Draw From The Deck — Only a seasoned adventurer may draw from the deck. During each player's fifth and subsequent turns, once during your precombat main phase, you may spend a Fate Point and draw a card from the Deck of Many Things.
• Optional Rule: When drawing from the deck, you may instead declare how many cards you wish to draw (1 to 4 cards) instead of one. Effects happen in the order drawn.
Optional Gameplay:
• Gaining Fate Points — When you have no Fate Points, you may choose to roll 1d6 during your main phase.
On a 6: Gain 1 Fate Point
If you would gain a Fate Point but already have one, you may instead draw a card from the Deck of Many Things.
Card Resolution — When a card is drawn from the Deck of Many Things, it is cast immediately without paying its mana cost. Drawing from the deck cannot be countered, doesn't use the stack, and can only be responded to by a card named The Fates.
• When a card drawn from the Deck of Many Things would go anywhere other than the battlefield, unless explicitly stated on the card as part of its mechanics, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things, except for cards named The Jester or The Fool.
• If multiple cards are drawn, resolve each card completely in sequence before moving on to the next card.
• Emblems created by the Deck of Many Things cannot be removed except by effects that specifically say they can remove emblems.
• The Legend rule does not apply to permanents drawn from the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: "Sometimes the cards you're dealt become the fate you're given."

cards.name: Soul Guardian
cards.rules_text:
Defender, Flying, Hexproof
Whenever Soul Guardian blocks a creature, that creature gets -2/-2 until end of turn.
Whenever a creature blocked by Soul Guardian dies during combat, that creature's controller discards a card.

cards.name: Soul Prison
cards.rules_text:
When Soul Prison enters, put a despair counter on it and create two 6/8 colorless Guardian Horror Spirit creature tokens named Soul Guardian.
Your soul is imprisoned. (You can't gain life, pay life costs, or activate non-land mana abilities. You can cast only one spell each turn, and those spells cost {X} more to cast, where X is the amount of despair counters on Soul Prison. You can play no more than one land each turn. You can't attack with creatures. Whenever a creature deals combat damage to you, it deals an additional 2 damage.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, put a despair counter on Soul Prison. Spells you cast cost an additional {1} for each despair counter on it.
At the beginning of each end step, if you control no Soul Guardians, sacrifice Soul Prison.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may sacrifice any number of creatures you control that aren't named Soul Guardian. If creatures with total combined power of 15 or greater were sacrificed this way, sacrifice Soul Prison.
At the beginning of your upkeep and if Soul Prison has three or more despair counters on it, you may have your life total become half its current value, rounded up. If you do, sacrifice Soul Prison and exile the top ten cards of your library.
cards.flavor_text: A fate worse than death—to watch eternity pass by, with memories slipping away.

cards.name: Temporal Stasis Sphere
cards.rules_text:
Tick Tock — When Temporal Stasis Sphere enters, roll a d4 and add 3 to the result. Put that many time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter and mill three cards. When the last time counter is removed, exile Temporal Stasis Sphere at the beginning of the next end step.
You are dimensionally displaced. (A dimensionally displaced player can't be affected by any effect in the main game. They remain in the main game's turn order but can't interact with or be interacted with by other players. Spells you cast or permanents you play are exiled. Starting with your next turn, skip your draw step on even-numbered turns. Your maximum hand size is reduced by 2.)
When this emblem leaves the battlefield, return and cast all cards exiled with it and The Donjon to play at the beginning of your next upkeep. For each card returned this way, lose 1 life.

cards.name: The Aberration
cards.rules_text:
Living weapon (When this Equipment enters the battlefield, create a 0/0 black Phyrexian Germ creature token, then attach this to it.)
Equipped creature gets +2/+2, has flying and intimidate.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may reveal the top card of target opponent's library. If you do, you may exile it face down. You may look at and cast spells exiled with The Aberration, and you may spend mana as though it were mana of any color to cast them.
Whenever equipped creature deals combat damage to a player, choose one —
• Look at the top X cards of that player's library, where X is the damage dealt. Put one of them into your hand and the rest into their graveyard.
• That player reveals their hand. You may cast a spell from it without paying its mana cost.
Equip {0}
cards.flavor_text: The voices whisper secrets you were never meant to know, and truths you cannot un-learn.

cards.name: The Beast
cards.rules_text:
When The Beast enters the battlefield, roll 2d12. Put that many time counters on it and create that many 2/2 green Beast creature tokens.
At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter. When the last counter is removed, sacrifice The Beast.
Creatures you control are green Beasts in addition to their other colors and types and have base power and toughness 2/2.
At the beginning of combat on your turn, roll 2d6. Creatures you control have base power and toughness equal to the result until end of turn.
Unleash — {0}, {T}: Until end of turn, creatures you control gain double strike and trample.
cards.flavor_text: Nature cares not for what you were—only what you become.

cards.name: The Book
cards.rules_text:
When The Book enters the battlefield, roll a d6 and add 2. The result is the number of experience counters you get.
{T}: Choose one —
• Enlighten — Exile a card from your hand: Put a page counter on The Book. For each page counter on The Book, you may play an additional land on each of your turns and draw an additional card during each of your draw steps.
• Comprehend — Look at the top X cards of your library, where X is the number of experience counters you have. You may cast one spell from among them without paying its mana cost if it shares a type with a card exiled with The Book. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.
cards.flavor_text: Within its ever-shifting pages lie the secrets of a thousand worlds, each written in tongues both ancient and unspoken.

cards.name: The Bridge
cards.rules_text:
The Bridge enters the battlefield with three time counters on it.
{1}, {T}, Remove a time counter from The Bridge: End the turn. Activate this ability anytime you could cast an instant.
(Exile all spells and abilities from the stack, including this ability. The player whose turn it is discards down to their maximum hand size. Damage wears off, and "this turn" and "until end of turn" effects end.)
When you remove a time counter from The Bridge, scry 2.
When the last time counter is removed, sacrifice The Bridge and take an extra turn. During that turn, you may play an additional land and draw two cards at the beginning of your draw step.
cards.flavor_text: Some bridges cross rivers or chasms. This one crosses the boundaries of time itself.

cards.name: The Campfire
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, put X Rest counters on The Campfire, where X is half the number of Rest counters on it, rounded up.
{T}, {W}: Put X Rest counters on The Campfire.
{T}, Remove any number of Rest counters: Choose one —
• Rejuvenate — Return target permanent card with mana value X or less from your graveyard to the battlefield, where X is the number of Rest counters removed this way.
• Invigorate — Put X +1/+1 counters divided as you choose among up to X target creatures, where X is the number of Rest counters removed this way.
• Recuperate — You gain X life and draw X cards, where X is the number of Rest counters removed this way. Skip your next turn.
cards.flavor_text: In its flames dance memories of peace long forgotten, a respite from the endless battles ahead.

cards.name: The Cavern
cards.rules_text:
The Cavern enters the battlefield tapped with three depth counters on it.
{T}: Add two mana in any combination of colors. If The Cavern has no depth counters on it, add four mana in any combination instead.
{T}, Remove a depth counter: Choose one —
• Target creature card in your hand gains perpetual "Suspend 1 — {0}" Then you may search your library for a land card, reveal it, and put it into your hand.
• Until end of turn, creatures you control gain deathtouch, reach, and "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may put a creature card from your hand onto the battlefield."
When the last depth counter is removed from The Cavern, creatures you control gain menace and creature spells you cast have convoke.
cards.flavor_text: In these ancient depths, the line between climbing up and down becomes meaningless—there is only the path forward.

cards.name: The Celestial
cards.rules_text:
Enchant creature
Living Aura (When this enchantment enters the battlefield, if you control no creatures, create a 0/0 colorless Spirit creature token, then attach this to it.)
Enchanted creature gets +4/+4, has flying, vigilance, becomes an Angel in addition to its other types, and has "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, exile target nonland permanent that player controls until The Celestial leaves the battlefield."
At the beginning of your upkeep, create two 4/4 white Angel creature tokens with flying and vigilance.
Whenever enchanted creature attacks, choose one —
• Until end of turn, Angels you control gain lifelink and double strike.
• Until end of turn, Angels you control gain indestructible and "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may draw a card."
cards.flavor_text: Not all who ascend are born with wings; some earn their place among the heavens.

cards.name: The Construct
cards.rules_text:
Propagate — When The Construct enters, put X 0/1 blue and black Homunculus artifact creature tokens into play, where X is the number of artifacts you control.
Whenever a Homunculus enters the battlefield under your control, each opponent loses 1 life. Then, if you control three or more Homunculi, draw a card.
{T}, Sacrifice a Homunculus: Choose one — Activate only as a sorcery.
• Replicate — Create a non-legendary token copy of The Construct.
• Dominate — Gain control of target creature with power less than or equal to the number of Homunculi you control until end of turn. Untap it. It becomes a Homunculus in addition to its other types and gains haste.
cards.flavor_text: In its creation, you glimpse infinity—in its eyes, you command it.

cards.name: Curse of Death's Door
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
When Curse of Death's Door enters the battlefield, enchanted player's life total becomes 0.
Enchanted player can't lose the game for having 0 or less life and can't gain life except from Curse of Death's Door.
At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, roll a d20:
• 1 | Get two death counters.
• 2–9 | Get one death counter.
• 10–19 | Get one life counter.
• 20 | Gain 1 life and sacrifice Curse of Death's Door.
When enchanted player receives damage from any source, they get one death counter.
When enchanted player has three or more death counters, they lose the game.
When enchanted player has three or more life counters, they gain 1 life and sacrifices Curse of Death's Door.
cards.flavor_text: Some corpses refuse to let go of life. Others refuse to let life go.

cards.name: The Crossroads
cards.rules_text:
Roll a twenty-sided die.
If the result is even — Each player's life total becomes equal to rolling a one-hundred-sided die.
If the result is odd — Target opponent and you exchange life totals, then roll two d10s. You gain life equal to the first roll and each opponent loses life equal to the second roll. This life loss can't reduce a player's life total below 1.
cards.flavor_text: Some paths lead forward, others back. All lead to change.

cards.name: The Door
cards.rules_text:
When The Door enters, if it wasn't a token, it gains eight portal counters.
Gate — {T}, Remove a portal counter: Choose one —
• Search your library for a land card and put it onto the battlefield tapped. Return it to your hand at the beginning of the next end step unless you remove a portal counter.
• Exile target permanent other than The Door. Return it to the battlefield under its owner's control at the beginning of the next end step unless you remove a portal counter.
• You may put a permanent card from your hand onto the battlefield. At the beginning of the next end step, return it to its owner's hand unless you remove a portal counter.
• Create a token that's a copy of target permanent. That token gains "When The Door leaves the battlefield, sacrifice this permanent" unless you remove a portal counter.
When the last portal counter is removed from The Door, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: Every doorway is a choice between here and elsewhere.

cards.name: The Dragon
cards.rules_text:
Devoid, Flying, Menace
The Dragon gains the following abilities based on other Dragons you control:
• White: Vigilance, protection from black and "Other Dragons you control get +2/+2 and have vigilance."
• Blue: Ward {0}, hexproof and "Other Dragons you control have ward {0} and can't be countered."
• Black: Deathtouch, lifelink and "Other Dragons you control have deathtouch and whenever they deal damage, each opponent loses 1 life."
• Red: Double strike and "Other Dragons you control have first strike and 'When this creature attacks, it deals 2 damage to any target.'"
• Green: Trample, reach and "Other Dragons you control get +3/+1 and have trample."
Whenever The Dragon attacks, create two 3/3 Dragon creature tokens with flying of a random color. They gain the associated ability.
Grouth — {T}, Sacrifice a Dragon: Put three +1/+1 counters on The Dragon and draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: Fate is not a line, but a tapestry—and I am every thread.

cards.name: The Elemental
cards.rules_text:
When The Elemental enters, choose a color.
You and permanents you control gain protection from the chosen color. (They can't be dealt damage, enchanted, blocked, or targeted by anything of the chosen color.)
{T}: Add three mana of the chosen color.
Spells you cast of the chosen color cost {1} less to cast.
Whenever you cast a spell of the chosen color, you may change the chosen color.
cards.flavor_text: To master an element is not to control it, but to become one with its eternal nature.

cards.name: The Expert
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
If damage would be dealt to you or a creature you control, prevent X of that damage and creatures you control have bushido X, where X is The Expert's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control deal combat damage to a player, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Dexterity
Creatures you control have first strike, haste, and flanking.
Prevention effects can't reduce damage dealt by creatures you control.
Level 3 — {3}: Dexterity
Creature spells you cast have flash.
Creatures you control have flying, hexproof, and "If this creature would be dealt damage, you may have it phase out instead."
Level 4 — {4}: Dexterity
Creatures you control have skulk and double strike.
Whenever a creature you control deals combat damage to a creature defending player controls, you may have it deal that much damage to another target creature defending player controls.

cards.name: The Fey
cards.rules_text:
Shroud, Vanishing 10 (This permanent enters with ten time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter from it. When the last is removed, sacrifice it.)
Shimmer — When The Fey enters, exile your hand and library. Create X 0/1 green and blue Faerie creature tokens with flying, where X is the number of cards exiled this way.
If you would draw a card while your library has no cards in it, skip that draw instead.
Whenever a Faerie you control leaves play, look at three cards at random exiled with The Fey. You may cast one of them at random this turn without paying its mana cost.
{T}, Sacrifice a Faerie: Exile target nonland permanent. Its controller reveals cards from the top of their library until they reveal a nonland permanent and puts it onto the battlefield. Put the rest on the bottom of their library. Then they shuffle.
If The Fey leaves play, shuffle all cards exiled with it into their owner's library.
cards.flavor_text: Time bends like a willow in the Feywild, and every moment is a gamble with eternity.

cards.name: The Fiend
cards.rules_text:
Flash, Flying
When The Fiend enters the battlefield, starting with you and proceeding in turn order, each player may bargain. Each player who doesn't bargain for the first time this way discards a card, loses 5 life, and mills three cards.
Then, starting with you and proceeding in turn order, each player may bargain again. Repeat this process until all players decline.
To bargain, choose a mode not yet chosen by you this turn and flip a coin:
• Power — Win: Put six +1/+1 counters on each creature you control. Lose: Sacrifice six creatures.
• Knowledge — Win: Draw six cards. Lose: Mill 6 cards.
• Wealth — Win: Search your library for six land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle your library. Lose: Sacrifice six lands.
• Vengeance — Win: Target player sacrifices six permanents. Lose: You sacrifice six permanents.
• Immortality — Win: You gain 66 life. Lose: Your life total is reduced to 6.
• Dominion — Win: Gain control of six target permanents target player controls. Lose: Target opponent gains control of six permanents you control.
Whenever The Fiend becomes the target of a spell or ability, is dealt damage, or if all players refused to bargain this turn, shuffle it into its owner's library.
cards.flavor_text: Pick your poison carefully, mortal. The greater the reward, the greater the fall.

cards.name: The Giant
cards.rules_text:
When The Giant enters, you gain 20 life.
Each creature you control has base power and toughness at least 10/10, trample, reach, and "This creature can block up to three creatures each combat and deals excess combat damage to attacking player while blocking."
If a source an opponent controls would deal damage to you or permanents you control, prevent 1 of that damage.
cards.flavor_text: "The good news is we can see the objective. The bad news is it can reach us too." —Scout's last report

cards.name: The Humanoid
cards.rules_text:
Choose one —
• Preserve — Stop drawing cards from the Deck of Many Things until end of turn. (Any remaining draws are skipped.)
• Continue — Draw cards from the Deck of Many Things equal to the number of remaining draws you have declared this turn plus one.
cards.flavor_text: Fate offers few moments of clarity—fewer still the wisdom to recognize them.

cards.name: The Juggernaut
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
Creatures you control get +0/+X, absorb X, and ward {X}, where X is The Juggernaut's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control are dealt damage and survive, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Constitution
Creatures you control have vigilance.
Creatures you control enter the battlefield with a shield counter on them.
Spells and abilities your opponents control can't have your creature's toughness reduced.
Level 3 — {3}: Constitution
Creatures you control have persist.
Whenever a creature you control is dealt damage and survives, put that many +1/+1 counters on it.
Spells and abilities your opponents control can't cause you to sacrifice permanents.
Level 4 — {4}: Constitution
Creatures you control have indestructible and "Whenever this creature attacks, defending player sacrifices a nonland permanent."

cards.name: The Lance
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, draw a card, gain X life, and create X 1/1 colorless Human Knight creature tokens with vigilance, where X is The Lance's level.
Level 2 — {2}: Attributes
Creatures you control have exalted and mentor.
At the beginning of your upkeep, put a +1/+1 counter on each creature you control.
Level 3 — {3}: Attributes
Whenever you attack, creatures you control get +X/+X until end of turn, where X is the number of attacking creatures you control.
At the beginning of your end step, proliferate.
Level 4 — {4}: Attributes
Whenever you attack for the first time each turn, untap all creatures you control. After this phase, there is an additional combat phase.
Whenever you proliferate, proliferate once more.

cards.name: The Mage
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, draw X cards. Spells you cast cost {X} less to cast, where X is The Mage's level.
Level 2 — {2}: Intelligence
Creatures you control have prowess and spells you cast can't be countered.
The first instant or sorcery spell you cast each turn has "When you cast this spell, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy."
Level 3 — {3}: Intelligence
Spells you cast have cascade.
Whenever you cast a spell, you may draw a card, then discard a card.
You may cast spells from your graveyard and from exile.
Level 4 — {4}: Intelligence
Spells you cast have storm.
Whenever you cast a spell from anywhere other than your hand, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy.
Creatures you control have protection from colored spells and abilities.
You have hexproof.

cards.name: The Map
cards.rules_text:
When The Map enters the battlefield, put three seek counters on it.
{T}, Remove a seek counter from The Map: Choose one —
• Locate — Name a card. Reveal cards from the top of target player's library until you reveal the named card. Put the named card from among them into your hand, shuffle the rest into that player's library. That player loses life equal to that card's mana value.
• Plunder — Look at target player's hand and choose a nonland card from it. That player discards that card. You may cast that card this turn and spend mana as though it were mana of any color to cast it. If you don't cast it this turn, exile it.
• Manifest — Search your library for an artifact, enchantment, or planeswalker card with mana value 4 or less and put it onto the battlefield. If it's a planeswalker, it enters with an additional loyalty counter on it. Then shuffle.
cards.flavor_text: Knowledge of a thing's true location grants power over its very existence.

cards.name: Curse of Exhaustion
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
When Curse of Exhaustion enters the battlefield, roll a d3. Put that many exhaustion counters on enchanted player.
For each exhaustion counter on enchanted player:
0 — Sacrifice Curse of Exhaustion.
1 — Skip your draw step unless you discard a card.
2+ Creatures you control get -2/-2.
3+ Spells you cast cost {1} more to cast and abilities you activate cost {1} more to activate.
4+ You lose half your life.
5+ Creatures you control can't attack or block.
6+ You lose the game.
At the beginning of your end step, if you haven't cast a spell or activated an ability this turn, remove an exhaustion counter. Otherwise, put an exhaustion counter on you.
cards.flavor_text: Each step forward is two steps lost in the endless corridors of fatigue.

cards.name: The Mine
cards.rules_text:
When The Mine enters the battlefield, roll 2d6 (gems), then roll 1d10 (ore). Create X Treasure tokens, where X is (gems x 5) plus (ore x 3).
Excavate — At the beginning of your upkeep, roll a d6. Create that many Treasure tokens.
Prospect — {T}, Sacrifice The Mine: Roll 2d6. Create twice that many Treasure tokens.
cards.flavor_text: Some veins run deeper than the earth itself.

cards.name: The Monstrosity
cards.rules_text:
When The Monstrosity enters the battlefield, roll a d20. Its base power and toughness each become the result of the roll plus 5. An opponent chosen at random gains control of it.
Haste, Trample, Menace
The Monstrosity can't block.
The Monstrosity attacks the player who drew it each combat if able until that player has 0 or less life or The Monstrosity leaves play. When either condition is met, shuffle The Monstrosity into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: Some horrors need no explanation—only survival.

cards.name: Gelatinous Cube
cards.rules_text:
Trample, Menace, Protection from instants and sorceries
When Gelatinous Cube enters, exile target creature you control and you become engulfed. (An engulfed player loses 2 life at the beginning of their upkeep, and must discard a card and mill three cards during their draw step. A player can't be engulfed more than once.)
Whenever Gelatinous Cube deals combat damage to a player, that player becomes engulfed. It gets +2/+2 for each engulfed player and must attack each combat if able.
Whenever Gelatinous Cube deals combat damage to a creature, exile that creature. Put a +1/+1 counter on Gelatinous Cube for each creature exiled with Gelatinous Cube.
When Gelatinous Cube leaves play, all players cease to be engulfed and all creatures exiled with Gelatinous Cube return to the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: Its translucent form belies a hunger older than stone.

cards.name: The Path
cards.rules_text:
Creatures you control have haste.
{T}: Add one mana of any color to your mana pool.
Whenever a land enters tapped under your control, untap it.
You may play an additional land on each of your turns.
Landfall — Draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: The world yields to those who know the way, though only for a brief time.

cards.name: The Pit
cards.rules_text:
When The Pit enters, put 60 depth counters on it. Then roll 6d6. You lose life equal to the total rolled, and The Pit deals 2 damage to each creature you control for every 10 depth counters it has and they each get a fallen counter until their next turn.
Creatures with fallen counters:
• Can't attack unless their controller pays {3} for each attacking creature.
• Have base power equal to half their original power, rounded down.
• Combat damage dealt to it is doubled.
Whenever a creature attacks you or enters the battlefield under your control, The Pit deals 2 damage to it for every 10 depth counters The Pit has, rounded down, and gets a fallen counter until their next turn.
At the beginning of your upkeep, remove 5 depth counters from The Pit. If The Pit has 0 or less depth counters, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: The ground beneath your feet was never as solid as you believed.

cards.name: The Plant
cards.rules_text:
When The Plant enters, search for library for a land card and put it onto the battlefield tapped. Then create a 0/1 green Plant creature token for each land you control.
{T}: Add {G}{G}{G}.
Landfall — Whenever a land you control enters, you may put a +1/+1 counter on each Plant creature you control.
{G}: Choose one — The Plant doesn't untap during its controller's next untap step.
• Overgrowth — Create X 0/1 green Plant creature tokens, where X is the number of lands you control.
• Verdant — Until end of turn, lands you control become 2/2 Forest creatures that are still lands and have "When this creature deals combat damage to a player, create a 0/1 green Plant creature token."
• Entangle — Target land becomes a 0/4 Plant creature with defender and "{T}: You may tap or untap target creature." It's still a land.
cards.flavor_text: The forest holds secrets for those who know how to listen.

cards.name: The Priest
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, scry X and you gain X life, where X is The Priest's level.
At the beginning of your end step, if you gained life this turn, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Wisdom
Creatures you control have lifelink.
Whenever you gain life, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature you control.
Level 3 — {3}: Wisdom
Whenever a creature enters the battlefield, you gain 1 life.
At the beginning of your end step, you may return target creature card with mana value X or less from your graveyard to the battlefield, where X is the amount of life you gained this turn.
Level 4 — {4}: Wisdom
Spells you cast have convoke.
Whenever you tap a creature to convoke a spell, you gain 2 life and put a +1/+1 counter on that creature.

cards.name: Curse of Restraint
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player, shroud
When Curse of Restraint enters, roll a d6 and add 2 to the result. Put that many bind counters on it and you become restrained. (You can't cast more than one spell each turn and can't activate more than one ability each turn. Artifacts, and enchantments you control lose all abilities except for Curse of Restraint. Spells you cast cost {W} more to cast for each bind counter on Curse of Restraint. Creatures you control can't attack unless you pay {0} for each creature attacking.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose X life, where X is the number of bind counters on Curse of Restraint.
Whenever a creature deals combat damage to you or a creature you control, it deals an additional 2 damage.
{0}, Pay X life and exile X cards from your hand or X nontoken permanents you control: Remove X bind counters from Curse of Restraint. X can't be greater than 2. Activate only as a sorcery.
When Curse of Restraint has no bind counters on it, sacrifice it.
Eternal Damnation — You can't draw from the Deck of Many Things for the rest of the game.
cards.flavor_text: Freedom is a distant memory when bound by forces beyond mortal understanding.

cards.name: The Ring
cards.rules_text:
Indestructible
When The Ring enters, roll a d20 put that many charge counters on it. You gain protection from everything until your next turn.
At the beginning of your upkeep, put two charge counters on The Ring.
{T}, Remove X charge counters: Choose one —
• Empower — Until end of turn, creatures you control get +X/+X and gain your choice of flying, vigilance, or trample.
• Burden — Draw X cards and lose X life.
• Corrupt — Target player loses X life and you gain X life.
cards.flavor_text: Some gifts are too powerful to resist, too precious to relinquish.

cards.name: The Shield
cards.rules_text:
Indestructible
Living Weapon (When this Equipment enters, create a 0/0 black Phyrexian Germ creature token, then attach this Equipment to it.)
Fortify — At the beginning of your upkeep or whenever equipped creature blocks or becomes blocked, you may put a fortification counter on The Shield.
Equipped creature gets +1/+3 for each fortification counter on The Shield.
Transmutation — {0}: Remove all abilities granted by The Shield from equipped creature. Choose two — Indestructible, Hexproof, Shroud, Protection from a color of your choice, Ward {6}, Absorb 3, Reach, Vigilance, Defender, Persist, Undying, Lifelink, or Regenerate. It gains the chosen abilities until end of turn.
Equip {0}
cards.flavor_text: Those who seek to break it teach it only new ways to be unbreakable.

cards.name: The Ship
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 2/2 blue, black, and red Human Pirate creature token with "When this creature enters the battlefield, if you control three or more Pirates, you may search your library for a land card, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle."
Whenever a Pirate you control attacks, choose an ability from among first strike, deathtouch, lifelink, or vigilance. That creature gains the chosen ability until end of turn.
Whenever a Pirate you control deals combat damage to a player, draw a card and create a Treasure token.
Crew 8 (Tap any number of creatures you control with total power 8 or more: This Vehicle becomes an artifact creature until end of turn.)
Whenever The Ship becomes crewed, choose three abilities from: flying, trample, lifelink, double strike, menace, hexproof, or annihilator 1. It gains those abilities until end of turn.
cards.flavor_text: Part of the crew, part of the ship.

cards.name: The Staff
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, put a dominion counter on The Staff.
{T}, Choose one — If The Staff has three or more dominion counters on it, you may choose any number instead and without paying their activation costs:
• {0}: Add three mana of any one color.
• {0}: Draw two cards, then discard a card.
• {0}: Target player loses 3 life and you gain 3 life.
• {0}: The Staff deals 3 damage to any target.
• {0}: Create a 3/3 blue Elemental creature token with flying.
• {0}: Return target card with mana value 3 or less from your graveyard to your hand.
• {0}: Counter target spell or activated ability or triggered ability.
• {0}: Exile target nonland permanent until The Staff leaves the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: In its crystalline core, reality itself bends to your will.

cards.name: The Stairway
cards.rules_text:
Choose one —
• Compel — Target player draws a card from the Deck of Many Things.
• Dictate — The next time a player would draw from the Deck of Many Things this game, they draw two cards from it instead and you choose which resolves first. Exile the other.
• Deny — The next time target player would draw a card from the Deck of Many Things this game, that player skips that draw instead.
• Wonder — Search your library for an artifact card and put it onto the battlefield. That artifact gains indestructible. Then shuffle your library.
cards.flavor_text: The stairway presents four paths: compulsion, mastery, denial, and wonder.

cards.name: Curse of Petrification
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player, shroud
When Curse of Petrification enters, you become petrified. (As long as you are petrified, you can't cast spells. Your creatures have indestructible, enter tapped, don't untap during your untap step, and can't attack. All damage that would be dealt to you or permanents you control by any source is reduced to 1. You can't activate abilities except abilities of Curse of Petrification. You can't receive poison counters.)
Whenever you're dealt damage, put an erosion counter on Curse of Petrification.
{G}, Pay X life or sacrifice X nontoken, nonland permanents: Put X erosion counters on Curse of Petrification. X can't be greater than 3. Activate only as a sorcery.
When Curse of Petrification has seven or more erosion counters on it, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: In stillness, eternity. In silence, preservation. In stone, waiting.

cards.name: The Tavern
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, create X 0/1 black and white Human Follower creature tokens with battle cry, where X is The Tavern's level.
Creatures you control have training.
Level 2 — {2}: Charisma
Creatures you control have "Whenever this creature becomes trained, draw a card.", and "Tap three untapped creatures you control: tap target creature an opponent controls."
Level 3 — {3}: Charisma
At the beginning of your upkeep, gain control of target creature an opponent controls with power less than or equal to the number of creatures you control until end of turn. It gains haste.
Whenever a creature enters the battlefield under your control, put a +1/+1 counter on it.
Level 4 — {4}: Charisma
Whenever a creature enters the battlefield under an opponent's control, it enters the battlefield under your control instead.

cards.name: Divine Intervention
cards.rules_text:
When Divine Intervention enters, exile it. You may cast it from exile without paying its mana cost anytime you could play an instant. If you do, choose one effect you haven't chosen this game:
• Return all permanent cards from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Your life total becomes your starting life total.
• Exile all nonland permanents your opponents control.
• Search your library for up to five cards and put them into your hand.
If you would lose the game while Divine Intervention is exiled, you may cast it from exile instead without paying its mana cost. If you do, your life total becomes equal to your starting life total, remove all poison counters from you, reset all commander damage dealt to you, and return target creature from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped.
When this card is cast from exile, after it resolves, shuffle it back into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: The covenant is sealed in starlight. Choose wisely when to call upon it.

cards.name: True Resurrection
cards.rules_text:
When True Resurrection enters, exile it. You may cast it from exile without paying its mana cost anytime you could play an instant. If you do, choose one —
• Choose a player who has lost this game. If that player lost more than one turn ago, they may return to the game with their life total equal to their starting life total, no poison counters, reset all commander damage dealt, draw 7 cards, put a land from their hand on the battlefield tapped, and skip their next draw step.
• Return target creature card from any graveyard to the battlefield under your control tapped. It gains "If this creature would leave the battlefield, exile it instead" and indestructible. Other effects that were attached or equipped on it when it died are also restored to the creature.
When this card is cast from exile, after it resolves, shuffle it back into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: Death holds no dominion over those marked by the Tomb's ancient power.

cards.name: The Tower
cards.rules_text: Reveal the top two cards of the Deck of Many Things. Choose one of those cards and cast it without paying its mana cost. Shuffle the other card into the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: Sometimes fate offers a choice. Choose wisely — the path not taken may have been your salvation... or your doom.

cards.name: The Tree
cards.rules_text:
Enchant land
Living Growth (When this enchantment enters the battlefield, if you control no lands, create a basic Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, or Forest land card token, then attach this to it.)
When The Tree enters, draw three cards and gain 8 life.
Enchanted land is a green Plant creature with base power and toughness 4/8 and has "At the beginning of your upkeep, put two +1/+1 counters on this creature and create a 0/1 green Plant creature token with reach and defender." It's still a land.
Other Plants you control get +1/+1 for each counter on enchanted creature.
When The Tree leaves play, create X 1/1 green Plant creature tokens with reach and defender, where X is the enchanted creature's toughness, then create a basic Forest land token.
cards.flavor_text: Some seek to escape death. Others choose to become life itself.

cards.name: Vengeful Revenant
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing 10 (This creature enters with ten time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter from it. When the last is removed, shuffle it into the Deck of Many Things. Time counters remain on it as it changes zones.)
Haste, Flying, Shadow
Vengeful Revenant's power and toughness are each equal to 2 plus the number of creature cards in all graveyards.
When Vengeful Revenant enters the battlefield, if no player is haunted by it, the player who drew this card becomes haunted by Vengeful Revenant. (That player gets an emblem with "At the beginning of your upkeep, sacrifice a creature. If you can't, you lose 1 life." A player can't have multiple haunted emblems.) An opponent chosen at random gains control of it.
Vengeful Revenant can't block and attacks the player haunted by it each combat if able until that player loses the game.
If Vengeful Revenant would leave play, return it to the battlefield at the next end step.
Or: Shuffle Vengeful Revenant into the Deck of Many Things. Any player may activate this ability. Only activate this ability if Vengeful Revenant is on the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: Born from the slaughter of an entire army, it bears the wrath of a hundred souls.

cards.name: The Warrior
cards.rules_text:
(Gain the next level as a sorcery to add its ability.)
Creatures you control get +X/+X, frenzy X, and rampage X, where X is The Warrior's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control fight, block, or become blocked, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Strength
Creatures you control have trample and riot.
At the beginning of combat on your turn, you may have target creature you control fight target creature an opponent controls.
Level 3 — {3}: Strength
Creatures you control have menace and deathtouch.
If a creature you control would deal combat damage, it deals double that damage.
Level 4 — {4}: Strength
Creatures you control have reach and "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a creature an opponent controls and it survives the damage dealt, exile that creature until its controller's next end step."
Each creature you control can both block an additional creature and block creatures as though it counted as two creatures blocking.

cards.name: Well of Minor Magics
cards.rules_text:
{T}: Choose three different abilities from among the following. You can't choose the same ability twice. Activate this ability only as a sorcery.
• Add one mana of any color.
• Scry 1 or Surveil 1.
• Look at target player's hand.
• Target player mills a card.
• Target player gains 1 life.
• Tap or untap target creature.
• Target creature gets +1/+1 until end of turn.
• Target creature gains haste until end of turn.
• Well of Minor Magics deals 1 damage to any target.
{T}: Activate one of the chosen abilities, then draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: Each ripple in its depths whispers secrets of a different art.

cards.name: The Avalanche
cards.rules_text:
Split Second (As long as this spell is on the stack, players can't cast spells or activate abilities that aren't mana abilities.)
Each player sacrifices X lands, where X is the number of lands they control minus 1.
All creatures get -8/-8 until end of turn and can't block this turn. If a creature would die this turn, exile it instead. At the beginning of the next end step, for each creature exiled this way, create a 0/1 colorless Wall creature token with defender and "When this creature dies, each player loses 1 life."
cards.flavor_text: "The earth doesn't care if you survive. The void beneath it hopes you don't."

cards.name: The Bear
cards.rules_text:
Trample
When The Bear enters the battlefield, put X +1/+1 counters on it where X is your devotion to green. (Each {G} in the mana costs of permanents you control counts toward your devotion to green.)
Whenever The Bear deals combat damage to a player or permanent, double the number of +1/+1 counters on it.
Pay 2 life: Put a +1/+1 counter on The Bear. Activate only during your turn.
cards.flavor_text: "To become the beast is to understand: nature's greatest strength lies not in teeth or claw, but in endless, unstoppable growth."

cards.name: Curse of Vulnerability
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
Vanishing 3 (This permanent enters with three time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter from it. When the last is removed, sacrifice it.)
Whenever a creature enchanted player controls blocks or becomes blocked by a creature an opponent controls, that creature gets -2/-2 until end of combat.
If damage would be dealt to enchanted player or creatures they control by a source an opponent controls, that damage is doubled.
cards.flavor_text: "In the end, it wasn't the pain that broke him. It was knowing there would always be more."

cards.name: The Betrayal
cards.rules_text:
Choose target opponent. Gain control of all creatures that player controls until end of turn and they gain haste. For each creature that changed control this way, create a Treachery token. (It's an enchantment token with "At the beginning of your upkeep, the chosen opponent loses 1 life." and "Vanishing 3".)
Whenever a creature you control that you don't own deals combat damage to a player this turn, exile the top card of that player's library. You may play that card this turn without paying its mana cost.
cards.flavor_text:
"Loyalty is a fragile thing, easily shattered by the right whisper."
— Kaya, the Betrayer

cards.name: The Big Sky
cards.rules_text: Each player reveals cards from the top of their library until they reveal seven land cards. Each player puts those lands onto the battlefield tapped under their control, then puts all other revealed cards on the bottom of their library in any order.
cards.flavor_text: "In the space between heartbeats, worlds fall like autumn leaves from the branches of possibility."

cards.name: The Brass Dwarf
cards.rules_text:
When The Brass Dwarf enters the battlefield, create three Forge tokens. (They're artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Create a token that's a copy of target artifact you control except it's also a 2/2 red and white Construct artifact creature with haste.")
Artifacts you control have "{T}: Add {R}."
Whenever you sacrifice an artifact, The Brass Dwarf deals 2 damage to any target. If the sacrificed artifact was a token, create a Treasure token.
cards.flavor_text: "In my forge, even scrap metal dreams of becoming masterworks."

cards.name: The Carnival
cards.rules_text:
When you cast The Carnival, exile the top nine cards of the Deck of Many Things. An opponent looks at those cards, turns them face down, and shuffles them. Choose one at random, reveal it, then cast it without paying its mana cost. Put the remaining exiled cards into the Deck of Many Things and shuffle.
Flip a coin. If you win the flip, repeat the above process. If you lose the flip, discard a card at random.
cards.flavor_text: "Behind these curtains lie nine fates. Choose carefully — or don't. The show must go on."

cards.name: The Courtesan
cards.rules_text:
Ward {W} (Whenever this creature becomes the target of a spell or ability an opponent controls, counter it unless that player pays {W}.)
When The Courtesan enters the battlefield, search your library for an Equipment card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
{W}: Until end of turn, target Equipment you control becomes an Artifact Creature — Construct with base power and toughness each equal to its mana value. It gains flying if its mana value is 4 or greater, deathtouch if its mana value is 3 or greater, and vigilance if its mana value is 2 or greater. Activate only as a sorcery.
Whenever an equipped creature you control deals combat damage to a player or dies, you gain 2 life, draw a card, and you may return it to its owner's hand.
cards.flavor_text: "In every blade sleeps a spirit. In every spirit sleeps a story."

cards.name: The Cricket
cards.rules_text:
When The Cricket enters the battlefield, you may choose to draw up to three additional cards from the Deck of Many Things. For each card drawn this way, put a Cricket counter on The Cricket.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may reveal the top X cards of your library, where X is the number of Cricket counters on The Cricket. Put any number of lands revealed this way onto the battlefield tapped, then put the rest on the bottom of your library in a random order.
Landfall — Draw X cards for each Cricket counter on The Cricket.
You may play an additional land on each of your turns for each Cricket counter on The Cricket.
cards.flavor_text: "Curiosity often leaps faster than caution."

cards.name: The Crows
cards.rules_text: Each player chooses a nonland permanent they control. Target opponent chooses one of those permanents. That permanent's controller sacrifices it. That player creates a Clue token and a 1/1 black Crow creature token with flying.
cards.flavor_text: "The crows care not for gold or flesh — only that a price is paid."

cards.name: Elder Air Elemental
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing 4 (This permanent enters with four time counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a time counter from it. When the last is removed, sacrifice this and exile all of the marked player's nonland permanents. That player planeswalks to the Plane of Air.)
Flash, Flying, Haste, Ward {W}
When The Cyclone enters, mark the player who summoned it, and an opponent chosen at random gains control of it.
Elder Air Elemental attacks the marked player each combat if able and can't block. As long as Elder Air Elemental is on the battlefield, other creatures and players can't attack or target the marked player.
cards.flavor_text: "Face me alone, mortal. Time grows short, and the winds of fate grow restless."

cards.name: The Dance
cards.rules_text:
If you would roll a die or flip a coin, instead roll two dice or flip two coins and choose one of those results.
Whenever you win a coin flip or roll a die using this ability, draw a card.
At the beginning of your end step, if you've drawn three or more cards this turn, you may sacrifice The Dance. If you do, take an extra turn after this one.
cards.flavor_text: "To master chance is to learn its rhythm, to feel its pulse, to know its steps."

cards.name: The Demon's Lantern
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, that player loses 1 life unless they pay {B}.
{B}: Choose target creature an opponent controls. That creature's controller may pay {B}. If they don't, exile that creature and create a Soul Gem artifact token with:
"When this artifact enters the battlefield, it gains a charge counter for each point of power the exiled creature had.",
"Remove a charge counter: Add one mana of any color.", and
"{B}, Pay X life, Sacrifice this artifact: Return the exiled creature to the battlefield under its owner's control, where X is that creature's mana value."
If The Demon's Lantern leaves the battlefield, destroy all Soul Gems.
cards.flavor_text: "The gem's light glows brighter with every whisper of despair."

cards.name: The Desert
cards.rules_text:
When The Desert enters the battlefield, put a drought counter on all lands.
Each land with a drought counter on it is a Desert and loses all other land types and abilities and has "{T}: Add {C}. Create a 0/1 colorless Sand creature token."
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player puts a drought counter on target non-Desert land they control.
Whenever a Sand creature dies, that creature's controller loses 1 life.
Sand creatures get +1/+0 for each Desert on the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: "First the wells dried. Then the rivers. Finally, the sky itself forgot the meaning of rain."

cards.name: The Demon
cards.rules_text:
Flying, Haste
When The Demon enters the battlefield, choose a player. That player becomes marked. (A marked player's maximum hand size is reduced by one for each doom counter on The Demon.)
Whenever The Demon deals combat damage to the marked player, put a doom counter on it and scry 1.
At the beginning of marked player's upkeep, The Demon deals damage to that player equal to the number of doom counters on it.
When The Demon has five or more doom counters on it, the marked player loses the game.
cards.flavor_text: "Your soul caught my eye. Soon, it shall catch my claws."

cards.name: The Foreign Trader
cards.rules_text:
Flash, Deathtouch
When The Foreign Trader enters the battlefield, you may pay any amount of life in multiples of 5. For each 5 life paid this way, create three Treasure tokens, put an age counter on it, and scry 1. If you don't, sacrifice it and create a Treasure token.
At the beginning of your upkeep, for each age counter on The Foreign Trader, mill three cards.
Your maximum hand size is increased by the number of age counters on The Foreign Trader and spells you cast cost {1} less to cast for each age counter on it.
Whenever you sacrifice a Treasure token, each opponent loses 1 life and you gain 1 life.
cards.flavor_text: "Time is the only currency that cannot be counterfeited. Shall we trade?"

cards.name: The Forge
cards.rules_text:
When The Forge enters the battlefield, create three Ember tokens. (They're artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Add {R}{R}.")
{T}, Sacrifice an Equipment card you control: Search your library for an Equipment card with equal or lesser mana value and put it onto the battlefield attached to a creature you control. That Equipment gains "Equipped creature gets +4/+4" and can't be equipped to other creatures. Then create an Ember token. Shuffle your library.
Whenever an Equipment enters the battlefield under your control, create a 3/1 red and white Spirit Artificer creature token with haste. It gains "When this creature dies, attach target Equipment you control to target creature you control."
Whenever you sacrifice an artifact, The Forge deals 2 damage to any target. If the sacrificed artifact was a token, deal an additional 2 damage.
cards.flavor_text: "In the heart of creation's flame, what was becomes what could be."

cards.name: The Eclipse
cards.rules_text:
When The Eclipse enters the battlefield, each player sacrifices all creatures they control. For each creature sacrificed this way, that player loses 1 life.
Whenever a player loses life, put that many tribute counters on The Eclipse. When there are forty or more tribute counters on The Eclipse, sacrifice it.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player sacrifices a nonland permanent. If they can't, they lose 3 life and discard a card. For each permanent sacrificed this way, its controller creates a 3/1 red Horror creature token with "When this creature leaves play, it deals 3 damage to you."
Creatures have "At the beginning of your end step, exile this creature unless you pay life equal to its power."
cards.flavor_text: "All things end. The Eclipse demands tribute."

cards.name: The Empty Throne
cards.rules_text:
When The Empty Throne enters, create five Treasure tokens.
Decree — {T}, Choose one —
• Prosperity — Create two Treasure tokens and gain 2 life.
• Nobility — Create two 1/1 white and black Noble creature tokens with "Other creatures you control get +1/+0."
• Ascension — Nobles you control gain flying and vigilance until the end of your next turn.
• Tribute — Each opponent loses life equal to the number of Nobles you control.
Nobles you control have "Whenever this creature attacks, create a Treasure token."
cards.flavor_text: "Power seeks those who do not seek it, for they might wield it wisely."

cards.name: The Hidden Truth
cards.rules_text: Look at target opponent's hand, library, or face-down cards they own in any zone. You may choose a nonland card from among them and exile it with three time counters on it. It gains "Remove a time counter from this card at the beginning of your upkeep. When the last is removed, you may cast this card without paying its mana cost. If you don't, you lose 3 life."
cards.flavor_text: "Perfect knowledge requires perfect understanding."

cards.name: The Idiot
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
Enchanted player plays with their hand revealed, skips their draw step, and can't cast spells during their main phases.
Mental Decay — At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, that player draws a card and exiles three cards from their hand face down. They may look at and play those cards as if they were in their hand.
Profound Confusion — Spells cast by enchanted player cost {1} more to cast. If a spell wasn't cast from exile with The Idiot, counter that spell.
Lost Wisdom — At the beginning of enchanted player's end step, if they have no cards in hand, they draw three cards, then discard three cards at random.
cards.flavor_text: "Once-sharp thoughts now scatter like leaves in a storm, never to be gathered again."

cards.name: The Inquisitor
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When The Inquisitor enters, look at target opponent's hand. Name a card. Choose a card at random from their hand. If it's the named card, exile that card. Until the end of your next turn, that player can't cast spells or activate abilities.
{T}, {W}, Sacrifice The Inquisitor: Look at target opponent's hand and choose up to three cards from it. That player exiles those cards. Until end of turn, you may cast one of the exiled cards without paying its mana cost. At the beginning of the next end step, return the other cards to their owner's hand.
cards.flavor_text: "Your secrets are but bargaining chips in our little game of truth."

cards.name: The Joke
cards.rules_text: Exile the top three cards of the Deck of Many Things face up. An opponent chosen at random may cast one of those cards at random without paying its mana cost. Then you may cast one of the remaining exiled cards without paying its mana cost. Remove the last exiled card from the game.
cards.flavor_text: "Three fates revealed, two embraced, one forever lost — even destiny has a sense of humor."

cards.name: The Juggler
cards.rules_text:
Flash, Haste
When The Juggler enters the battlefield, exile the top three cards of your library face up. For as long as they remain exiled, you may cast spells from among them, and you may spend mana as though it were mana of any color to cast them.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may return any number of exiled cards you own to your library, then exile that many cards from the top of your library face up.
Whenever you cast a spell from exile, copy it and create a token copy of The Juggler. You may choose new targets for the copy.
cards.flavor_text: "Reality is merely another ball to keep in the air."

cards.name: The Keep
cards.rules_text:
When The Keep enters the battlefield, exile any number of nonland permanents you control. Those cards gain "You may cast this card from exile."
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may return one card exiled with The Keep to the battlefield under your control.
{2}, {T}: Exile target permanent you control. It gains "You may cast this card from exile."
cards.flavor_text: "Within its ethereal walls, reality bends to the will of its master."

cards.name: The Liar
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When The Liar enters the battlefield, look at each opponent's hand. Choose a nonland card from each of those hands. Exile the chosen cards face down.
When The Liar attacks, you may cast any card it exiled without paying its mana cost. If you do, after that spell resolves, its owner draws three cards and discards three cards. Spells you cast this way can't be countered.
cards.flavor_text: "Truth bends like light through crystal — and I am the one who holds the prism."

cards.name: The Locksmith
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When The Locksmith enters the battlefield, choose one —
• Unlock — Until your next turn, target permanent loses all abilities and can't be sacrificed.
• Duplicate — Create a colorless Key artifact token with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Target permanent can't attack, block, or activate abilities until end of turn."
{1}, {T}: Put a key counter on target artifact, creature, or enchantment. (Permanents with key counters have "{T}: Add one mana of any color.")
{5}, {T}, Remove all key counters from among permanents you control: Gain control of target permanent with mana value less than or equal to the number of key counters removed this way. Activate only as a sorcery and only once per game.
cards.flavor_text: "Every barrier has a weakness. Every lock has a solution. I've mastered them all."

cards.name: The Lost
cards.rules_text:
All non-Spirit creatures lose all abilities and can't gain abilities. They can't transform or have counters put on them.
If a non-Spirit creature would die, exile it instead. That creature's controller creates a 1/1 black and green Spirit creature token with "This creature's power and toughness are each equal to the number of Spirits you control."
Whenever a Spirit enters, its controller gains 1 life and each other player loses 1 life.
Whenever a non-Spirit creature enters, its controller loses 2 life and each other player gains 2 life.
cards.flavor_text: "Some paths, once abandoned, lead only to stranger roads."

cards.name: The Marriage
cards.rules_text:
When The Marriage enters the battlefield, target player gains control of target nonland permanent you control, then you gain control of target nonland permanent they control. Put a blessing counter on each of those permanents.
Permanents with blessing counters have hexproof, vigilance, and "At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 1/1 white and green Spirit token with 'Whenever this creature attacks, put a blessing counter on another target permanent you control.'"
At the beginning of each end step, if you control four or more permanents with blessing counters, you may remove all blessing counters from permanents you control. If you do, search your library for any number of permanent cards with total mana value X or less, where X is twice the number of counters removed this way, and put them onto the battlefield. They enter with blessing counters on them.
cards.flavor_text: "In union, we find strength neither could possess alone."

cards.name: The Midwife
cards.rules_text:
Flash
Whenever a permanent not named The Midwife enters the battlefield under your control, put a birth counter on it.
When The Midwife enters, create two 1/1 white green Spirit creature tokens with lifelink.
Permanents you control with birth counters on them have "At the beginning of your upkeep, create a non-legendary token that's a copy of this permanent."
cards.flavor_text: "Life begets life, each birth a miracle that echoes eternally."

cards.name: The Mountain Man
cards.rules_text:
Haste, Trample, Ward {2}
When The Mountain Man enters or attacks, you may search your library for up to two land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
The Mountain Man's power and toughness are each equal to twice the number of lands you control, rounded down.
Landfall — The Mountain Man deals damage equal to half the number of lands you control, rounded down, to any target.
cards.flavor_text: "The peaks do not yield to those who climb them. They yield to those who become them."

cards.name: The Mute Hag
cards.rules_text:
Hexproof
When The Mute Hag enters the battlefield, target opponent chooses sight, hearing, or speech at random from among the types they don't already have a deprivation counter for, then they receive a deprivation counter of the chosen type. (The types are sight, hearing, and speech.)
• A player with a sight counter is blind and can't look at cards in any zone except the battlefield.
• A player with a hearing counter is deaf and can't activate nonmana abilities.
• A player with a speech counter is mute and can't cast spells during other players' turns.
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, if that player has one or more deprivation counters, they lose 2 life for each deprivation counter they have unless they sacrifice that many permanents. Then, that player gains a deprivation counter of a type chosen at random from among the types they don't already have.
{B}: Remove a deprivation counter. Any player may activate this ability.
cards.flavor_text: "In the absence of one sense, terror fills all others."

cards.name: The Owl
cards.rules_text:
Flash
Flying, Ward {2}
Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, scry 1.
Whenever you scry, you may pay {1}. If you do, gain 2 life and draw a card.
{T}, Sacrifice The Owl: Scry 7, then target opponent may scry 3. Draw a card.
When The Owl dies, investigate. (Create a Clue token. It's an artifact with "{2}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw a card.")
cards.flavor_text: "Perfect knowledge comes with perfect awareness."

cards.name: The Paladin
cards.rules_text:
Flying, Double Strike, Vigilance
When The Paladin enters the battlefield, choose one —
• You create five 2/2 white Knight creature tokens with vigilance.
• You gain 10 life and draw three cards.
• You get an emblem with "Creatures you control have protection from the color of your choice and lifelink."
Whenever The Paladin attacks, exile target nonland permanent an opponent controls until The Paladin leaves the battlefield.
At the beginning of your end step, return target permanent card from your graveyard to the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: "To serve justice is to wield both sword and shield — one to protect the innocent, one to strike down evil."

cards.name: The Peacock
cards.rules_text:
When The Peacock enters the battlefield, creatures you control gain indestructible until end of turn.
Creatures you control get +0/+4 and have hexproof.
Creatures can't attack you unless their controller pays {2} for each creature they control that's attacking you.
